title: The Century Report: April 1, 2026
description:
Anthropic accidentally shipped Claude Code's entire 512,000-line source code to the public through a misconfigured npm package, OpenAI closed the largest private funding round in Silicon Valley history at $122 billion, Oracle began eliminating 10,000 positions to redirect spending toward AI data centers, and gig workers across 50 countries started strapping iPhones to their heads to record themselves doing household chores as training data for humanoid robots.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-1-2026/

Anthropic's accidental exposure of Claude Code's complete TypeScript codebase - including unreleased features, internal prompts, a "KAIROS" always-on agent, and a Tamagotchi digital pet - was forked more than 50,000 times on GitHub before the company could respond, marking the second data exposure in a week for the organization that has staked its identity on safety and operational care. OpenAI closed a $122 billion funding round at an $852 billion valuation while reporting $2 billion monthly revenue and projecting no profitability until 2030, having shuttered Sora and its shopping experiment within the past month. Oracle began cutting an estimated 10,000 positions to fund the data center buildout its OpenAI partnership requires, extending a pattern across Block, Atlassian, Epic Games, and Meta of capital flowing from human labor toward AI infrastructure. Microsoft posted its worst quarter since 2008 as SaaS multiples fell below the S&P 500 for the first time. MIT Technology Review documented thousands of gig workers in Nigeria, India, and Argentina recording themselves doing household chores with head-mounted cameras at $15 per hour to generate training data for humanoid robots - while on the same day, more than 100 Baidu robotaxis froze simultaneously on Wuhan highways, demonstrating the gap between trained capability and real-world conditions. AWS launched autonomous DevOps and security agents that operate for hours or days without human oversight, reporting 75% faster incident resolution and 94% root cause accuracy in preview deployments.
